EXTENSIVE FLOODING REPORTED ACROSS NORTHERN AND EASTERN FRANKLIN COUNTY. BIGGEST PROBLEMS BETWEEN ROCKY MOUNT AND SMITH MOUNTAIN LAKE. NINE VEHICLES STRANDED IN FLOOD WATERS ALONG VA 122. TWO 60-INCH CULVERTS WASHED OUT AT THE WINDY RIDGE FARM SUBDIVISION WITH CURRENTLY NO ACCESS IN/OUT OF THE SUBDIVISION FOR THE 15 RESIDENTS LIVING THERE. OTHER ROADS WITH MINOR DAMAGE AND UNDERMINED BY THE FLOOD WATERS. REPORTS OF RAINFALL IN EXCESS OF 4 IN/2 HRS ACROSS THIS PART OF THE COUNTY.
SIX TO EIGHT INCHES OF WATER FLOWING OVER A BRIDGE ON POWELLS STORE ROAD

AN UNNAMED STREAM CAME OUT OF ITS BANKS AND FLOODED SPINNAKER WAY AND HARBOUR TRAIL...LEAVING BEHIND DEBRIS ON THE ROADS. OVER 6 INCHES OF WATER FLOWED OVER THE ROADS. NUMEROUS DRIVEWAYS AND FRONT LAWNS WERE FLOODED.

STRONG WINDS CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO A HOME...WITH ROOF SHINGLES BLOWN DOWN AND PORCH SCREENS BLOWN IN. A LARGE LIMB WAS BLOWN OUT OF A NEARBY TREE.

NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ACROSS THE COUNTY. MOST COUNTY ROADS EXPERIECED TREES BLOWN ACROSS THEM. ONE CHURCH IN HINTON HAD A PORTION OF ITS ROOF PEELED OFF FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS. AT LEAST SIX HOMES HAD TREES FALL ON THEM AND AT LEAST SIX VEHICLES WERE DAMAGED BY TREES FALLING ON THEM. RADAR ESTIMATES SUGGEST THE STRONGEST WINDS EXITED THE COUNTY AROUND 830 PM.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ON HOUSES IN RAINELLE...LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT ON THE SCENE
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SPOTTERS HOUSE...AND MULTIPLE 85-FOOT HIGH WHITE BIRCH TREES DOWN

IN MONROE CONTY...HUNDREDS OF TREES BLOWN DOWN...POWER LINES BLOWN DOWN. WORST DAMAGE OCCURRED NORTH AND WEST OF UNION. SEVERAL VEHICLES WERE DAMAGED BY FALLING TREES. IN LINDSIDE...ONE PERSON SUSTAINED SCRAPES AND BRUISES WHEN A TREE FELL ON THE VEHICLE THEY WERE DRIVING...BUT WAS NOT TRANSPORTED TO A MEDICAL FACILITY. RADAR ESTIMATES THE STRONGEST WINDS EXITED MONROE COUNTY AROUND 840 PM.
DAMAGE TO A TIN ROOF, TREE BRANCHES 4 TO 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER DOWN IN ALDERSON.

HUNDREDS OF TREES AND POWER LINES BLOWN DOWN ACROSS BATH COUNTY. ONE TREE FELL ON A HOME AND A FEW TREES FELL ON CARS CAUSING DAMAGE. ONE PERSON HAD DEBRIS BLOWN IN THEIR EYE...BUT WAS NOT TAKEN TO A MEDICAL FACILITY. RADAR ESTIMATED THE STRONGEST WINDS EXITED THE COUNTY AROUND 850 PM.
TREE DOWN ON HOUSE AT BLAZER PLACE AND SOUTH STATE STREET.
3 TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN CROSSING GARDNER ROAD.

NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. A CHURCH ROOF WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS. A PORTION OF THE HIGH SCHOOL ROOF AND A ROOF AT A LOCAL HOTEL WAS PEELED OFF BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS. SEVERAL VEHICLES WERE DAMAGED WHEN TREES WERE BLOWN ON THEM AND TREES BLEW DOWN ON SOME HOMES. ACROSS ALLEGHANY COUNTY...HUNDREDS OF TREES BLOWN DOWN...SOME VEHICLES DAMAGED. RADAR ESTIMATES THE STRONGEST WINDS EXITED ALLEGHANY COUNTY AT 852 PM...AND STRUCK COVINGTON AROUND 835 PM.

WIND GUST MEASURED AT THE GREENBRIER VALLEY AIRPORT

IN CRAIG COUNTY HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN...POWER LINES DOWN. A FEW BARNS WERE DAMAGED...A PORCH WAS DESTROYED BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS ON ROUTE 42. SEVERAL VEHICLES WERE DAMAGED WHEN WINDS BLEW TREES DOWN ON THEM. RADAR DATA ESTIMATES THE STRONG WINDS EXITED CRAIG COUNTY AROUND 853 PM.

*** 12 INJ *** HUNDREDS OF TREES BLOWN DOWN COUNTYWIDE. POWER LINES ALSO BLOWN DOWN. LESS THAN 20 STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED...MAINLY FROM WINDS BLOWING OFF ROOFING. TREES FELL ON SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES IN BLUE RIDGE. FOUR TO FIVE PEOPLE WERE INJURED FROM FLYING DEBRIS AT THE BUCHANAN CARNIVAL. TWO PEOPLE WERE INJURED WHEN A VEHICLE SWERVED TO AVOID A FALLING TREE AND CRASHED INTO ANOTHER VEHICLE. THREE PEOPLE WERE INJURED AT THE MIDDLE CREEK CAMPGROUND AND THREE OTHERS WERE TAKEN TO MEDICAL FACILITIES. ENDED 843PM
THE ROOF OF A HOME WAS BADLY DAMAGED BY STRONG WINDS ON MUANEY ROAD.

NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES ACROSS THE COUNTY...STARTING AT 845 PM AND LASTING UNTIL ABOUT 915 PM. ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE BARN...OTHERWISE NO KNOWN SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE.
NUMEROUS LARGE TREES DOWN AND FENCING BLOWN DOWN.

*** 2 INJ *** HUNDREDS OF REPORTS OF TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. TWO OR THREE PEOPLE WERE INJURED AT AN OUTDOOR SHOW AT NATURAL BRIDGE. CARS WERE CRUSHED BY FALLING TREES IN BUENA VISTA...TREES FELL ON HOMES IN LEXINGTON AND A GARAGE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF IN FANCY HILL. ESTIMATES FROM RADAR SHOW STRONGEST WINDS EXITED ROCKBRIDGE COUNTY AROUND 1010 PM.
AT LEAST A DOZEN LARGE TREES AND HUNDREDS OF MEDIUM TO LARGE SIZE LIMBS DOWN ALONG PEARMAN ROAD IN PRESTON FOREST...ESPECIALLY AT ELEVATIONS ABOVE 2200 FT.

HUNDREDS OF REPORTS OF TREE AND POWER LINE DAMAGE ACROSS MONTGOMERY COUNTY WERE CALLED IN TO THE 911 CENTER FRIDAY NIGHT FROM THE EXTREME WINDSTORM THAT ENTERED THE COUNTY AROUND 850PM AND LASTED THROUGH ABOUT 920 PM. NUMEROUS REPORTS OF STRUCTURAL DAMAGE...MAINLY FROM TREES FALLING ON HOMES...WERE ALSO REPORTED. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES WERE REPORTED IN ASSOCIATION WITH THE STORM. HOWEVER...ONE FATALITY OCCURRED HOURS LATER WHEN A MOTORCYCLIST DROVE INTO A TREE BEING REMOVED FROM A ROAD.

ACROSS THE COUNTY THERE WERE HUNDREDS OF REPORTS OF TREES DOWN...AS WELL AS POWER LINES AND NUMEROUS POWER OUTAGES. THIS OCCURRED BETWEEN 855 PM AND 915 PM. IN DUBLIN...SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES WERE ALSO DESTROYED. THERE WERE NO REPORTS OF INJURIES IN THE COUNTY.

HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ACROSS ROANOKE COUNTY AS WELL AS NUMEROUS POWER LINES DOWN. IN THE SOUTHEAST PORTION OF ROANOKE COUNTY AN UPROOTED TREE FLIPPED A MOBILE HOME. MANY HOMES HAD SHINGLES AND SIDING BLOWN OFF. THE MOST CONCENTRATED AREA OF DAMAGE EXTEDNED FROM FORT LEWIS TO HOLLINS TO VINTON. THE STRONGEST WINDS EXITED AROUND 915 PM.
